_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Security management involves identifying and protecting an organisation's assets (systems, people and buildings).
reference_document_element_description: Build and lead cross-functional transformation teams, establishing shared objectives, working norms, and accountability structures across organizational boundaries.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-T-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-K-003 (LS, cosine: 0.437), AB-K-007 (AB, cosine: 0.428).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 1.0/10 (raw 0.104). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DDaT-only terms [assets, buildings, identifying, involves, management, organisation] vs WIDAI-only terms [accountability, across, boundaries, build, cross, establishing] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.862 (P=0.870, R=0.854). Balanced P/R — the 13-word DDaT element and 17-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (100.0%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Security management involves identifying and protecting an organisation's assets" and "Build and lead cross-functional transformation teams, establishing shared object". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.311 > STS 0.104 (gap +0.207). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.5%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DDaT terms: [assets, buildings, identifying, involves, management, organisation]. WIDAI terms: [accountability, across, boundaries, build, cross, establishing].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 100.0% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.207 with low STS 0.104. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
